pytorch.reason: not needed -- no autograd layer in this proof sim

z3.reason: Load-bearing: proves UNSAT for each channel family -- no stricter linear function separates admissible from inadmissible. Also proves quadratic coefficient is identically zero (linearity). Negative test: 2-qubit depolarizing NONLINEAR boundary is SAT.

cvc5.reason: Supportive: cross-checks the depolarizing linear boundary via independent SyGuS synthesis -- confirms boundary is f = λ + p - 1, not quadratic.

sympy.reason: Load-bearing: analytically derives the admissibility boundary for each channel family from Kraus completeness + Bloch contraction. Checks that the boundary formula has zero quadratic coefficient.

summary.theorem_statement: All single-parameter qubit channel families have LINEAR admissibility boundaries in the (p, channel_param) plane: f = channel_param + p - 1.

amplitude_damping.physical_interpretation: Kraus completeness: K0†K0+K1†K1=I iff γ in [0,1]. Boundary saturates when total amplitude-damping budget p*γ exhausted at p+γ=1.

phase_damping.physical_interpretation: Same Kraus structure as amplitude damping (different Bloch action). x,y-contraction factor (1-γ) in [0,1] iff γ in [0,1]. Boundary: γ + p - 1 = 0 (identical to amplitude damping).

depolarizing.physical_interpretation: Depolarizing: (1-λ)ρ + λI/2. Bloch contraction 1-4λ/3. CPTP iff λ in [0, 3/4]. Normalized λ' = λ/(3/4) in [0,1]. Boundary: λ' + p - 1 = 0. Matches L4 lego result.

bit_flip.physical_interpretation: Bit flip: (1-p_f)ρ + p_f X ρ X†. Bloch: x->x, y,z -> (1-2p_f)*y,z. CPTP iff p_f in [0,1]. Boundary: p_flip + p - 1 = 0.

phase_flip.physical_interpretation: Phase flip: (1-p_ph)ρ + p_ph Z ρ Z†. Bloch: z->z, x,y -> (1-2p_ph)*x,y. CPTP iff p_ph in [0,1]. Boundary: p_phase + p - 1 = 0.

sympy_boundary_derivations.theorem_statement: All five single-parameter qubit channel families have LINEAR admissibility boundaries: f(p, θ) = θ + p - 1 = 0, where θ is the channel parameter. The quadratic cross-term θ*p is identically zero in all cases.

proof1_tightest_separator.interpretation: No linear function through boundary corners of amplitude_damping can be strictly tighter than θ+p-1=0 without misclassifying an admissible interior point. The linear boundary IS the tightest separator.

proof2_no_quadratic_needed.interpretation: θ*p cannot exceed 1/4 inside the admissible region θ+p<=1. The linear constraint alone fully controls the quadratic term. No quadratic boundary correction is needed for amplitude_damping.

proof1_tightest_separator.interpretation: No linear function through boundary corners of phase_damping can be strictly tighter than θ+p-1=0 without misclassifying an admissible interior point. The linear boundary IS the tightest separator.

proof2_no_quadratic_needed.interpretation: θ*p cannot exceed 1/4 inside the admissible region θ+p<=1. The linear constraint alone fully controls the quadratic term. No quadratic boundary correction is needed for phase_damping.

proof1_tightest_separator.interpretation: No linear function through boundary corners of depolarizing can be strictly tighter than θ+p-1=0 without misclassifying an admissible interior point. The linear boundary IS the tightest separator.

proof2_no_quadratic_needed.interpretation: θ*p cannot exceed 1/4 inside the admissible region θ+p<=1. The linear constraint alone fully controls the quadratic term. No quadratic boundary correction is needed for depolarizing.

proof1_tightest_separator.interpretation: No linear function through boundary corners of bit_flip can be strictly tighter than θ+p-1=0 without misclassifying an admissible interior point. The linear boundary IS the tightest separator.

proof2_no_quadratic_needed.interpretation: θ*p cannot exceed 1/4 inside the admissible region θ+p<=1. The linear constraint alone fully controls the quadratic term. No quadratic boundary correction is needed for bit_flip.

proof1_tightest_separator.interpretation: No linear function through boundary corners of phase_flip can be strictly tighter than θ+p-1=0 without misclassifying an admissible interior point. The linear boundary IS the tightest separator.

proof2_no_quadratic_needed.interpretation: θ*p cannot exceed 1/4 inside the admissible region θ+p<=1. The linear constraint alone fully controls the quadratic term. No quadratic boundary correction is needed for phase_flip.

test_2qubit_nonlinear.name: 2qubit_depolarizing_nonlinear_boundary
test_2qubit_nonlinear.interpretation: 2-qubit depolarizing CAN have λ1+λ2 > 1 while still being admissible (product contraction >= 0). This VIOLATES the linear boundary theorem. Confirms: linear boundary only holds for SINGLE-parameter qubit channels. 2-qubit channels require a NONLINEAR (quadratic) boundary: admissibility condition involves λ1*λ2 cross-term.

test_single_qubit_boundary_strict.name: single_qubit_linear_boundary_strict
test_single_qubit_boundary_strict.interpretation: λ > 3/4 with |1-4λ/3| <= 1 is UNSAT. Single-qubit depolarizing linear boundary λ = 3/4 is strict -- no admissible channel exceeds it.

boundary.note: Linear boundary f=θ+p-1 correctly classifies all test points. Interior (f<0), boundary (f=0), forbidden (f>0). All five channel families share this identical classification structure.

cvc5_crosscheck.name: cvc5_depolarizing_boundary_crosscheck
cvc5_crosscheck.interpretation: cvc5 synthesized: (lambda ((lambda Real) (p Real)) (- (+ p lambda) 1.0)). Boundary IS linear. Cross-check with z3 linear boundary proof: AGREES.
